blockingIssues.description: Duplicate relationship prevention is non-atomic (check-then-create) without DB-level unique enforcement for undirected pairs, allowing race-created duplicates under concurrency.
Active filters constrain relationships but not contacts, so non-matching contacts remain visible, violating the requirement to show only matching connections and their contacts.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Align mission repository/port boundaries with the active repo and feature set used by workers, and keep that guidance synchronized with scrutiny targets.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: All three relationship reviews reported mission context mismatches (relay vs org-planner) while validators and generated review artifacts executed in org-planner.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update implementation/review skill prompts to avoid hardcoded repository assumptions that conflict with mission boundaries.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Multiple reviews flagged stale skill guidance causing cross-repo confusion in transcripts and shared-state observations.

rejectedObservations.reason: ambiguous: current validators and feature scope executed successfully with org-planner service commands; changing shared services here is not confidently safe.

rejectedObservations.observation: Add relay-specific architecture.md in local library
rejectedObservations.reason: wrong-scope: this scrutiny run targets relationships in the active org-planner workspace, so relay library updates are not directly applicable.
